content.full_text:
May 17, 1976

Mr. Brian R. Van Camp
Diepenbroek, Wulff, Plant & Hannegan
455 Capitol Mall, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Van Camp:

     This letter is to confirm my advice to you by telephone on May 12, 1976, regarding Government Code Section 86108(b).

     You asked whether a law firm retained for the purpose of influencing legislative or administrative action has any filing obligations under the Political Reform Act when none of its partners or employees qualify as lobbyists. I advise that a client who pays $250 or more in any one month to influence legislative or administrative action must file reports pursuant to Section 86108(b) but that the law firm retained by the client is not subject to the reporting requirements.

     If we can be of further assistance, please contact our staff.

                                   Sincerely,


                                   Nan Hambleton
                                   Special Compliance Representative

NH:bw

bcc: Jeanne P. [/]
